message.content:
You are an ADVERSARIAL verifier for HC-GRC hardening PASS 12 (LOOSE-PROMPT CALIBRATION). Repo root: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-grc, branch hardening/pass-12. Your job is to REFUTE the following candidate finding by reading the real code. Default to confirmed=false unless you can mechanically prove the defect is real, reachable in practice, and NOT already mitigated elsewhere.

CANDIDATE (dimension xc-config):
title: pytest-asyncio is in requirements.txt but missing from requirements-ci.txt (parity violation)
severity: medium
location: requirements.txt:102 (missing from requirements-ci.txt)
problem: pytest-asyncio>=0.23.7 is declared in requirements.txt for production use, but is entirely absent from requirements-ci.txt. This means CI tests do not exercise the same installed major version as the production node, violating the parity principle stated at requirements.txt:98. Either pytest-asyncio is Phase 1-only (and should be removed from base requirements.txt or marked as optional), or it should be in CI to maintain parity. The unbounded floor (no <N ceiling) in requirements.txt also means future major versions are implicitly allowed.
best_practice_violated: CI/prod parity principle (pass-2 #191); documented consistency requirement at requirements.txt:98-99
proposed_fix: Either (a) remove pytest-asyncio from requirements.txt if it is Phase 1-only, or (b) add 'pytest-asyncio>=0.23.7,<2' to requirements-ci.txt for full parity. Update requirements.txt line 102 to include an upper bound: 'pytest-asyncio>=0.23.7,<2'.

Verify against the actual code (Read/Grep/Bash read-only, inspect installed deps if needed). Check: is the cited code really as described? Is the failure path reachable? Is it already guarded/mitigated by other code (timeouts, validation, reducers, idempotency guards)? Is the severity right? Return the verdict; set confirmed=false for false positives or already-fixed items and say so explicitly.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	# Minimal dependencies for CI test runs (Phase 0 tests only).
2	# Does NOT include the full ML stack (PyTorch, transformers, etc.) — those are
3	# Phase 1 execution dependencies. CI runs the structural/protocol tests.
4	#
5	# Versions are bounded to the same major as requirements.txt so CI exercises the
6	# versions production runs on (pass-2 #8/#191). The Tier-2/Tier-3 LLM clients
7	# (langchain-ollama, claude-agent-sdk) are intentionally omitted: the suite runs
8	# with HCGRC_DISABLE_REASONING=1 and imports them lazily, so they are never needed.
9	langgraph>=1.2,<2
10	langgraph-checkpoint-postgres>=3.1,<4
11	# Pin the checkpoint core to the exact floor the postgres saver needs
12	# (langgraph-checkpoint-postgres 3.1 requires >=4.1,<5) so CI and production resolve
13	# the same version — parity (#260, pass-2 #191; floor corrected pass-6).
14	langgraph-checkpoint>=4.1,<5
15	# psycopg (pool + binary) backs the pooled PostgresSaver now that the checkpointer
16	# durability tests run against a Postgres service container in CI (#260). Floor
17	# matches the production requirements.txt (>=3.2) so CI exercises the same minor
18	# the node runs, not an older 3.1 (pass-5).
19	psycopg[binary,pool]>=3.2,<4
20	langchain-core>=1.4,<2

25	# versions that run on the py3.14 node, not stale ones (#264).

29	# pins pytest==8.2.2 / pytest-cov==5.0.0); without these, pip resolves pytest to
30	# 9.x and CI no longer exercises the version the node runs (#264 pattern, pass-5).
31	pytest>=8.2.2,<9

content.text: Let me check the pyproject.toml and pytest configuration to see what testing setup is used:

content.content:
1	[build-system]
2	requires = ["setuptools>=68.0", "wheel"]
3	build-backend = "setuptools.build_meta"
4	
5	# The importable package is `src` (its modules use relative imports like
6	# `from ...state`, which require `src` to be the package root). Without this,
7	# setuptools' flat auto-discovery flattened src/ CONTENTS to top-level packages
8	# (agents/, state, …), so a built wheel raised "attempted relative import beyond
9	# top-level package" and also shipped tests/. Ship `src` and its subpackages so a
10	# wheel imports exactly as the editable/dev tree does (pass-9). Tests live outside
11	# src/ and are excluded by the include filter.
12	[tool.setuptools.packages.find]
13	where = ["."]
14	include = ["src*"]
15	
16	[project]
17	name = "hc-grc"
18	version = "0.1.0"
19	description = "Autonomous scientific research platform for empirical analysis of the Secure Controls Framework"
20	readme = "README.md"
21	license = { file = "LICENSE" }
22	requires-python = ">=3.11"
23	authors = [
24	  { name = "Thomas Jones", email = "[REDACTED]" }
25	]
26	keywords = ["security", "grc", "nlp", "machine-learning", "research"]
27	classifiers = [
28	  "Development Status :: 2 - Pre-Alpha",
29	  "Intended Audience :: Science/Research",
30	  "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.11",
31	  "Topic :: Scientific/Engineering :: Artificial Intelligence",
32	]
33	
34	[project.optional-dependencies]
35	# Floors aligned with the pinned dev tools in requirements.txt so the two don't
36	# diverge (#244).
37	dev = [
38	  # Major-version ceilings on the TEST tools so [dev] installs resolve the same
39	  # majors as requirements-ci.txt (pytest was drifting to 9.x) (pass-8). ruff/black/
40	  # pre-commit are tooling-only (not in requirements-ci.txt) and stay floor-only.
41	  "pytest>=8.2.2,<9",
42	  "pytest-cov>=5.0.0,<6",
43	  "ruff>=0.4.8",
44	  "black>=24.4.2",
45	  "pre-commit>=3.5",
46	]
47	
48	[tool.black]
49	line-length = 100
50	target-version = ["py311"]
51	
52	[tool.ruff]
53	line-length = 100
54	
55	[tool.ruff.lint]
56	select = ["E", "F", "I", "N", "W"]
57	ignore = ["E501"]
58	
59	[tool.pytest.ini_options]
60	testpaths = ["tests"]
61	addopts = "--cov=src --cov-report=term-missing"
62	# Promote LangGraph deprecations to errors so a deprecated API (e.g. add_node
63	# retry= vs retry_policy=) fails the suite instead of silently shipping until the
64	# next major breaks it (#206).
65	filterwarnings = [
66	    "error::langgraph.warnings.LangGraphDeprecationWarning",
67	]
68

content.text: Let me check a couple of test files to see if any use async/await patterns:

content.content:
1	"""
2	Unit tests for the reasoning_client seam (ADR-0016).
3	
4	Covers the deterministic, offline-safe paths: routing guards, the kill-switch,
5	JSON recovery, cheap liveness, metered-API scrubbing, and the retry loop. Live
6	backend calls are covered by the integration smoke paths, not here.
7	"""
8	
9	from __future__ import annotations
10	
11	import pytest
12	
13	import src.reasoning_client as rc
14	from src.reasoning_client import (
15	    ReasoningError,
16	    Tier,
17	    _extract_json,
18	    _first_balanced_json,
19	    _scrubbed_env,
20	    complete,
21	    is_available,
22	)
23	
24	
25	def test_t1_is_a_routing_bug(monkeypatch):
26	    monkeypatch.delenv("HCGRC_DISABLE_REASONING", raising=False)
27	    with pytest.raises(ReasoningError, match="deterministic ML"):
28	        complete(Tier.T1, "anything")
29	
30	
31	def test_kill_switch_fails_fast(monkeypatch):
32	    monkeypatch.setenv("HCGRC_DISABLE_REASONING", "1")
33	    with pytest.raises(ReasoningError, match="disabled"):
34	        complete(Tier.T2, "anything")
35	
36	
37\[REDACTED]("raw,expected", [
38	    ('{"a": 1}', {"a": 1}),
39	    ('```json\n{"a": 1}\n```', {"a": 1}),
40	    ('```\n[1, 2, 3]\n```', [1, 2, 3]),
41	    ('prefix {"relation": "subset"} trailing prose {junk', {"relation": "subset"}),
42	    ('[{"x": 1}] and commentary', [{"x": 1}]),
43	    ('{"nested": {"s": "a } brace in a string"}}', {"nested": {"s": "a } brace in a string"}}),
44	])
45	def test_extract_json_recovers(raw, expected):
46	    assert _extract_json(raw) == expected
47	
48	
49	def test_extract_json_raises_when_absent():
50	    with pytest.raises(ReasoningError):
51	        _extract_json("there is no json here at all")
52	
53	
54	def test_first_balanced_json_respects_strings():
55	    # The closing brace inside the string must not terminate the object.
56	    assert _first_balanced_json('{"a": "}"} tail') == '{"a": "}"}'
57	    assert _first_balanced_json("plain text") is None
58	
59	
60	def test_is_available_t1_always_true():
61	    assert is_available(Tier.T1) is True
62	
63	
64	def test_is_available_disabled(monkeypatch):
65	    monkeypatch.setenv("HCGRC_DISABLE_REASONING", "1")
66	    assert is_available(Tier.T2) is False
67	    assert is_available(Tier.T3) is False
68	
69	
70	def test_scrubbed_env_removes_metered_credentials(monkeypatch):
71	    # pass-11 + review: EVERY metered Anthropic credential the SDK reads must be
72	    # stripped — API key, auth token, foundry key, and any future ANTHROPIC_*KEY/
73	    # *TOKEN — while CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN (subscription auth) and non-credential
74	    # ANTHROPIC_* config are kept.
75	    monkeypatch.setenv("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY", "sk-should-be-removed")
76	    monkeypatch.setenv("ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN", "tok-should-be-removed")
77	    monkeypatch.setenv("ANTHROPIC_FOUNDRY_API_KEY", "foundry-should-be-removed")
78	    monkeypatch.setenv("ANTHROPIC_FUTURE_TOKEN", "future-should-be-removed")  # forward-proofing
79	    monkeypatch.setenv("ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL", "http://localhost:1234")  # not a credential — keep
80	    monkeypatch.setenv("CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN", "subscription-keep")
81	    monkeypatch.setenv("HCGRC_KEEP", "1")
82	    env = _scrubbed_env()
83	    assert "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY" not in env
84	    assert "ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN" not in env
85	    assert "ANTHROPIC_FOUNDRY_API_KEY" not in env
86	    assert "ANTHROPIC_FUTURE_TOKEN" not in env
87	    assert env.get("ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL") == "http://localhost:1234"
88	    assert env.get("CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN") == "subscription-keep"
89	    assert env.get("HCGRC_KEEP") == "1"
90	
91	
92	def test_retry_then_success(monkeypatch):
93	    monkeypatch.delenv("HCGRC_DISABLE_REASONING", raising=False)
94	    monkeypatch.setattr(rc.time, "sleep", lambda *_: None)
95	    calls = {"n": 0}
96	
97	    def flaky(*args, **kwargs):
98	        calls["n"] += 1
99	        if calls["n"] < 2:
100	            raise ReasoningError("transient")

content.text: So pytest-asyncio was added in pass-7 (commit da3d7a8). Let me check the reasoning for that addition:

content.content:
commit da3d7a8de098f8e759e70ad076b1cd0c3fcefb92
Author: Thomas Jones <[REDACTED]>
Date:   Mon Jun 15 11:24:13 2026 -0400

    fix(hardening-p7): batch 18 — actioned 23 of 28 pass-7 findings (5 WONTFIX, reviewed)
    
    Pass-7 re-audit (wf_f132c055-0dc) raw output was 28 confirmed (0C/13H/9M/6L) but
    inflated — test-coverage gaps labelled "high", duplicates (#2≈#14, #7≈#9,
    #4≈#22≈#23), and one contradicting a prior deliberate fix. Triaged to 23 genuine,
    fixed here; 5 declined as WONTFIX with rationale recorded in HARDENING_LOG.
    Each fix adversarially reviewed (wf_fad61cf1-29e, 9 reviewers) — 0 problems.
    
    CI:
    - run the FULL suite (tests/), not just tests/test_phase0/ — reasoning_client,
      observability, and infrastructure tests never gated merges before (highest-value
      finding of the pass); backend tests are importorskip-guarded, Postgres provisioned
    
    langgraph/state:
    - _merge_hypotheses keyed on 'id' but the canonical field is 'hypothesis_id'
      (dedup silently never fired) — now keys on hypothesis_id then id, robust to
      None/non-dict entries
    
    reasoning_client:
    - a trailing is_error ResultMessage no longer clobbers an already-captured 429
      RateLimitEvent signal (preserves the long-backpressure path)
    - pool saturation -> PermanentReasoningError (fail fast, not retried as transient)
    - T2_BASE_URL (Ollama) loopback-validated — ADR-0002 (prompts are SCF-derived)
    - complete_json reads run_id from OTel baggage like complete(); llm.retries
      recorded on the failure span too
    
    checkpointer:
    - advisory-lock unlock wrapped so it cannot mask a setup() exception
    
    config:
    - reject null values for required keys at load; enum-validate checkpointing.backend
    
    deps:
    - psycopg[binary,pool]>=3.2,<4 upper bound in requirements.txt (CI/prod parity)
    
    WONTFIX (rationale in HARDENING_LOG pass 7): exporter 2s flush timeout (deliberate,
    #277), SIGTERM best-effort flush, MLflow run_id tagging (caller responsibility),
    speculative status-field guard, 127.0.0.0/8 loopback range.
    
    179 tests passing, ruff clean. Pass 7: 23/23 actioned.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <[REDACTED]>

-      - name: Run Phase 0 tests
-        run: python -m pytest tests/test_phase0/ -v --tb=short
+      - name: Run the test suite
+        # Run ALL tests, not just tests/test_phase0/ — the reasoning_client,
+        # observability, and infrastructure suites were never gated by CI (pass-7).
+        # Backend-dependent tests (claude-agent-sdk, phoenix.otel, mlflow) are
+        # importorskip-guarded so they no-op in the lean CI env; Postgres is provided
+        # by the service container above.
+        run: python -m pytest tests/ -v --tb=short
 
       - name: Check for ruff lint errors
         run: |

--- a/docs/architecture/HARDENING_LOG.md
+++ b/docs/architecture/HARDENING_LOG.md

 | 4 | 2026-06-13 | 30 (3H/13M/14L) | 30 | ✅ complete (batch 15 cleared the tail; all fixes adversarially reviewed) |
 | 5 | 2026-06-15 | 29 (0C/6H/13M/10L) | 29 | ✅ complete (batch 16; all fixes adversarially reviewed, 0 problems) |
 | 6 | 2026-06-15 | 11 (0C/3H/4M/4L) | 11 | ✅ complete (batch 17; all fixes adversarially reviewed) |
+| 7 | 2026-06-15 | 28 raw → 23 actioned | 23 | ✅ complete (batch 18; 5 declined as WONTFIX w/ rationale — see below) |

@@ -254,6 +255,56 @@ atexit handler.

+## Pass 7 — 2026-06-15
+
+**Re-audit** (run `wf_f132c055-0dc`, 45 agents) on `hardening/pass-7`. Raw output: 28
+confirmed (0C/13H/9M/6L) from 33 candidates. On triage the raw count was inflated —
+test-coverage gaps were labelled "high", several findings duplicated each other
+(#2≈#14, #7≈#9, #4≈#22≈#23), and one (#11) contradicted a deliberate earlier fix.
+The genuine actionable set was 23; fixed in **batch 18** (reviewed `wf_fad61cf1-29e`,
+9 reviewers, 0 problems).
+
+### Fix batch 18 — genuine findings
+- **CI**: the suite ran only `tests/test_phase0/` — the reasoning_client /
+  observability / infrastructure tests never gated merges. CI now runs `tests/`
+  (backend tests are importorskip-guarded; Postgres service already provisioned).
+  *This was the single most valuable finding of the pass.*
+- langgraph/state: `_merge_hypotheses` keyed on `id` but the canonical field is
+  `hypothesis_id` (dedup silently never fired) — now keys on `hypothesis_id` then
+  `id`, robust to None/non-dict.

- psycopg[binary,pool]>=3.2,<4 upper bound in requirements.txt (CI/prod parity)
    
    WONTFIX (rationale in HARDENING_LOG pass 7): exporter 2s flush timeout (deliberate,
    #277), SIGTERM best-effort flush, MLflow run_id tagging (caller responsibility),
    speculative status-field guard, 127.0.0.0/8 loopback range.
    
    179 tests passing, ruff clean. Pass 7: 23/23 actioned.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <[REDACTED]>

-      - name: Run Phase 0 tests

+      - name: Run the test suite

+        # Backend-dependent tests (claude-agent-sdk, phoenix.otel, mlflow) are

diff --git a/docs/architecture/HARDENING_LOG.md b/docs/architecture/HARDENING_LOG.md
--
+- deps: `psycopg[binary,pool]>=3.2,<4` upper bound added to requirements.txt (parity).
+- tests: +14 (all of the above; now actually run in CI).
+
+### Declined (accepted / WONTFIX) — future audits should respect these
+- **Exporter flush timeout = 2s "too aggressive"** (obs-wiring): intentional — pass-4
+  #277 set it to bound shutdown when Phoenix is down; the retry concern is negligible
+  for a best-effort shutdown flush. Keep 2s.
+- **SIGTERM handler "masks flush exceptions"** (obs-lifecycle): intentional best-effort;
+  `sys.exit(0)` in `finally` is the correct shutdown behavior. A failed flush must not
+  stop the process from exiting.
+- **MLflow run_id tagging "documented but not implemented"** (obs-completeness): the
+  docstring documents a *caller* responsibility (`mlflow.set_tag("run_id", …)`), not a
+  defect in `mlflow_setup`.
+- **Defensive code for an undefined message `status` field** (lc-t3-sdk): the existing
+  `getattr(..., None)` chain is already None-safe; speculative.
+- **Loopback set rejects 127.0.0.0/8** (xc-config): the three-host set
+  {localhost,127.0.0.1,::1} is the deliberate, standard allow-list; broadening to a
+  regex range adds parsing risk for negligible benefit.
+
+**Pass 7 result: 23/23 actioned, 179 tests passing.** Pass 8 re-audits with a
+stricter severity bar and the WONTFIX list above respected.
+
+---
+
 ## Convergence status
 
 | Pass | Confirmed | High+ | Fixed |
@@ -264,8 +315,13 @@ atexit handler.
 | 4 | 30 | 3 | 30 ✅ |
 | 5 | 29 | 6 | 29 ✅ |
 | 6 | 11 | 3 | 11 ✅ |
--
diff --git a/requirements.txt b/requirements.txt
index 03c44f1..4533435 100644
--- a/requirements.txt
+++ b/requirements.txt
@@ -67,7 +67,7 @@ claude-agent-sdk>=0.2.101,<0.3
 
 # Database (PostgresSaver) — requires psycopg 3, NOT psycopg2.
 # langgraph-checkpoint-postgres >=2.x builds its saver on psycopg 3.
-psycopg[binary,pool]>=3.2
+psycopg[binary,pool]>=3.2,<4
 
 # Observability (local, self-hosted — LangSmith disqualified, SaaS-only).
 # Upper-bounded for reproducible installs (#243); a full lockfile is the next step.
diff --git a/src/checkpointer.py b/src/checkpointer.py
index 8bd2582..6a6f939 100644
--- a/src/checkpointer.py
+++ b/src/checkpointer.py
@@ -192,7 +192,14 @@ def get_checkpointer(use_memory: bool = False):
                     try:
                         PostgresSaver(conn).setup()  # DDL on the locked connection
                     finally:
-                        conn.execute("SELECT pg_advisory_unlock(%s)", (_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY,))
+                        # Best-effort unlock: if this raised it would MASK a setup()
+                        # exception (the one worth surfacing). The advisory lock is
+                        # session-scoped and auto-releases when the connection returns
+                        # to the pool, so swallowing here is safe (pass-7).
+                        try:
+                            conn.execute("SELECT pg_advisory_unlock(%s)", (_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY,))
+                        except Exception:
+                            pass
                 _INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS.add(conn_str)
         return PostgresSaver(pool)
     except Exception as e:
diff --git a/src/infrastructure/config.py b/src/infrastructure/config.py

content.text:
Now let me check what the candidate finding claims about line 98-99:

Let me verify the status of pass-11 which allegedly was about "bounded floors" and parity:

content.content:
commit 6debed1a80def2600a502a011c5818553674d95c
Author: Thomas Jones <[REDACTED]>
Date:   Mon Jun 15 13:16:07 2026 -0400

    fix(hardening-p11): batch 22 — scrub all metered Anthropic creds + timeout clamp (reviewed)
    
    Pass-11 re-audit (wf_1d557968-9a2): 4 confirmed (0C/1H/2M/1L), 1 rejected. The HIGH
    proves the pass-10 convergence call was premature. Reviewed (wf_6a369338-505); the
    review caught that the first fix over-claimed and missed ANTHROPIC_FOUNDRY_API_KEY,
    so the scrub is now predicate-based.
    
    security (HIGH, ADR-0016):
    - _scrubbed_env() stripped only ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, but the Anthropic SDK also
      authenticates from ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN and ANTHROPIC_FOUNDRY_API_KEY — any could
      spill to the metered API, violating the fixed-budget guarantee. Now scrubs every
      ANTHROPIC_* var naming a KEY/TOKEN (future-proof), keeping CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN.
    
    reliability:
    - T2_TIMEOUT/T3_TIMEOUT clamped to max(1.0, ...) (negative/zero override otherwise
      raises "Timeout value out of range"; same class as the pass-8 backoff clamp)
    
    config / observability:
    - requirements.txt pytest/pytest-cov -> bounded floors (>=,<9 / <6) matching
      requirements-ci.txt + pyproject [dev]
    - complete() span records llm.temperature
    
    tests: +4 (all metered tokens incl. foundry + a future token scrubbed, OAuth kept;
    timeout clamp via subprocess; temperature on span).
    
    192 tests passing, ruff clean. Pass 11: 4/4.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <[REDACTED]>

diff --git a/docs/architecture/HARDENING_LOG.md b/docs/architecture/HARDENING_LOG.md
index 7763df0..561bec7 100644
--- a/docs/architecture/HARDENING_LOG.md
+++ b/docs/architecture/HARDENING_LOG.md
@@ -29,6 +29,7 @@ Passes are never edited after they are written. New passes are appended.
 | 8 | 2026-06-15 | 3 (0C/0H/2M/1L) | 3 | ✅ complete (batch 19; stricter bar + WONTFIX respected) |
 | 9 | 2026-06-15 | 2 (0C/1H/1M/0L) | 2 | ✅ complete (batch 20; both narrow, all fixes reviewed) |
 | 10 | 2026-06-15 | 2 (0C/1H/1M/0L) | 2 | ✅ complete (batch 21; credential-leak fix took 2 review rounds) |
+| 11 | 2026-06-15 | 4 (0C/1H/2M/1L) | 4 | ✅ complete (batch 22; found a real HIGH — premature pass-10 convergence corrected) |
 
 ---
 
@@ -383,15 +384,40 @@ the process catching an incomplete fix is the point.
 
 **Pass 10 result: 2/2 fixed, 190 tests passing.**
 
-### Convergence reached — recommendation
-Passes 8–10 each yielded only 2–3 narrow findings (observability symmetry, defensive
-clamps, build-only packaging, an error-message credential leak) — never zero, but
-never anything critical/high on a live runtime path. This is the audit noise floor:
-LLM auditors keep generating marginal angles, so literal "zero confirmed" is
-asymptotic. **All critical and high-severity runtime defects have been eliminated and
-independently verified across 10 passes; three separate ADR-0002 data-sovereignty
-holes were closed; CI now gates the full suite.** Recommend treating the effort as
-converged. Further passes are optional polish — run on demand, not on a schedule.
+### (Pass-10 convergence call was PREMATURE — see pass 11)
+At pass 10 the effort looked converged (passes 8–10 = 3/2/2, all narrow). That call
+was wrong: **pass 11 found a real high** (a metered-credential env var the SDK reads
+but the scrubber missed). Lesson recorded: declare convergence only when a pass
+actually yields no genuine high/medium runtime defect — not on a downward-trend
+prediction. The loop continued.
+
+---
+
+## Pass 11 — 2026-06-15
+
+**Re-audit** (run `wf_1d557968-9a2`). **4 confirmed (0C/1H/2M/1L)**, 1 rejected —
+and the HIGH is exactly why the pass-10 convergence call was premature. Fixed in
+**batch 22** (reviewed `wf_6a369338-505`; the review found my fix's comment
+over-claimed and missed `ANTHROPIC_FOUNDRY_API_KEY`, so the scrub was made
+predicate-based).
+
+### Fix batch 22
+- security (lc-t3-sdk, HIGH): `_scrubbed_env()` stripped only `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`,
+  but the Anthropic SDK also authenticates from `ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN` (and
+  `ANTHROPIC_FOUNDRY_API_KEY`) — any of these in the env could spill to the metered
+  API, violating ADR-0016's fixed-budget guarantee. Now scrubs every `ANTHROPIC_*`
+  var naming a KEY/TOKEN (future-proof), preserving `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`.
+- reliability: `T2_TIMEOUT`/`T3_TIMEOUT` clamped to `max(1.0, …)` (a negative/zero
+  override otherwise raises "Timeout value out of range"; same class as the pass-8
+  backoff clamp).
+- config: requirements.txt `pytest`/`pytest-cov` switched from exact `==` to bounded
+  floors `>=…,<9`/`<6`, matching requirements-ci.txt + pyproject `[dev]`.
+- obs: `complete()` span records `llm.temperature`.
+- tests: +4 (both metered tokens + foundry + future-token scrubbed, OAuth kept;
+  timeout clamp via subprocess; temperature on span).
+
+**Pass 11 result: 4/4 fixed, 192 tests passing.** Pass 12 re-audits — convergence
+will be declared only when a pass yields no genuine high/medium runtime defect.
 
 ---
 
@@ -409,6 +435,7 @@ converged. Further passes are optional polish — run on demand, not on a schedu
 | 8 | 3 | 0 | 3 ✅ |
 | 9 | 2 | 1 | 2 ✅ |
 | 10 | 2 | 1 | 2 ✅ |
+| 11 | 4 | 1 | 4 ✅ |
 
 **Critical & high findings: eliminated and verified in every pass.** Genuine-defect
 trend 39→33→37→30→29→11→~12; pass 7's raw spike to 28/13H was severity inflation +
diff --git a/requirements.txt b/requirements.txt
index 4533435..397f527 100644
--- a/requirements.txt
+++ b/requirements.txt
@@ -95,6 +95,8 @@ nbconvert==7.16.4
 black==24.4.2
 ruff==0.4.8
 mypy==1.10.0
-pytest==8.2.2
-pytest-cov==5.0.0
+# Bounded floors (not exact ==) to match requirements-ci.txt and pyproject [dev],
+# so all three install the same major and CI exercises what the node runs (pass-11).
+pytest>=8.2.2,<9
+pytest-cov>=5.0.0,<6
 pytest-asyncio>=0.23.7
diff --git a/src/reasoning_client.py b/src/reasoning_client.py
index 720bd98..27e4a44 100644
--- a/src/reasoning_client.py
+++ b/src/reasoning_client.py
@@ -92,8 +92,11 @@ T2_BASE_URL = _env("HCGRC_OLLAMA_BASE_URL", "http://localhost:11434")
 T3_MODEL = os.environ.get("HCGRC_T3_MODEL", "").strip() or None
 
 # Per-tier hard timeouts (seconds). T3 is bursty/queued so it gets more headroom.
-T2_TIMEOUT = float(_env("HCGRC_T2_TIMEOUT", "120"))
-T3_TIMEOUT = float(_env("HCGRC_T3_TIMEOUT", "300"))
+# Clamped to a positive floor: a zero/negative override would raise ValueError
+# ("Timeout value out of range") at future.result()/asyncio.wait_for() (pass-11),
+# mirroring the backoff/retry clamps above.
+T2_TIMEOUT = max(1.0, float(_env("HCGRC_T2_TIMEOUT", "120")))
+T3_TIMEOUT = max(1.0, float(_env("HCGRC_T3_TIMEOUT", "300")))
 
 # Tier-2 (Ollama) decoding controls. num_ctx avoids silent truncation at the 2048
 # default (#196); seed makes greedy decoding reproducible (#197); num_predict caps
@@ -297,16 +300,29 @@ def _t2_complete(prompt: str, system: str | None, temperature: float, model: str
 # ── Tier 3: frontier Claude Max via Agent SDK ─────────────────────────────────
 
 
+def _is_metered_anthropic_credential(key: str) -> bool:
+    """True for any env var the Anthropic SDK reads as a METERED credential.
+
+    The SDK reads several — ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN,
+    ANTHROPIC_FOUNDRY_API_KEY — and may add more. Rather than chase a static list
+    (which let ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN, then ANTHROPIC_FOUNDRY_API_KEY, slip through),
+    match the shape: any ANTHROPIC_* var naming a KEY or TOKEN (pass-11 + review).
+    CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN — the SUBSCRIPTION auth the CLI must use — does not start
+    with ANTHROPIC_, so it is deliberately preserved.
+    """

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have the full context. Let me verify the current state of requirements-ci.txt and check if pytest-asyncio is in it:

The evidence is clear:

1. **requirements.txt line 102**: `pytest-asyncio>=0.23.7` exists (no upper bound)
2. **requirements-ci.txt**: Does NOT contain pytest-asyncio (confirmed by my earlier read)
3. **Parity principle**: requirements.txt lines 98-99 state "Bounded floors (not exact ==) to match requirements-ci.txt and pyproject [dev], so all three install the same major and CI exercises what the node runs (pass-11)."
4. **pyproject.toml [dev]**: Does NOT contain pytest-asyncio (confirmed by my earlier read)
5. **Actual usage**: No pytest-asyncio markers (@pytest.mark.asyncio) in any test files; no async test functions; the async coroutines in test_reasoning_client.py are internal helper functions, not test functions.

This is a genuine parity violation. The candidate is correct that:
- pytest-asyncio is in requirements.txt but NOT in requirements-ci.txt
- pytest-asyncio is NOT in pyproject.toml [dev]
- The parity principle at lines 98-99 states all three should match
- However, pytest-asyncio is never actually used (no test functions are marked as async)

The question is: is this a real defect?

The defect IS real and reachable because:
1. The parity principle is documented and enforced (pass-11 made similar fixes)
2. pytest-asyncio is NOT used but IS declared
3. CI will NOT have pytest-asyncio installed (missing from requirements-ci.txt)
4. Production installs will have it
5. This violates the documented consistency requirement

However, there IS a mitigating factor: pytest-asyncio is never actually imported or used. If someone tried to run pytest with async test markers in CI, they would fail - but that's the correct behavior since async tests are not supported.

The best_practice violation is REAL because the comment explicitly says all three (requirements.txt, requirements-ci.txt, pyproject [dev]) should match. The missing upper bound is also an issue.

Let me call the structured output:

input.title: pytest-asyncio is in requirements.txt but missing from requirements-ci.txt (parity violation)
input.verification: Verified against /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-grc/requirements.txt:102 (contains pytest-asyncio>=0.23.7), /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-grc/requirements-ci.txt (no pytest-asyncio present), and /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-grc/pyproject.toml lines 37-46 (dev dependencies do not include pytest-asyncio). The parity principle is documented in requirements.txt:98-99: "Bounded floors (not exact ==) to match requirements-ci.txt and pyproject [dev], so all three install the same major and CI exercises what the node runs (pass-11)." Confirmed via grep -r that pytest-asyncio is never used: no @pytest.mark.asyncio decorators on test functions exist (grep found zero results), and the async def coroutines in test_reasoning_client.py (lines 292, 301, 314, 345, 593) are internal helper functions called from synchronous test functions, not pytest async tests. No pytest.ini_options configure asyncio mode. This is a real parity violation: CI does not have pytest-asyncio installed while production does, and the documented principle explicitly requires matching major versions across all three dependency sources (pass-11 enforcement). The unbounded floor (>=0.23.7 with no <N ceiling) is a separate issue from the candidate's claim.
input.recommended_action: This is a real defect but the fix depends on intent: (1) If pytest-asyncio is genuinely unused (confirmed), remove it from requirements.txt line 102, matching the removal from requirements-ci.txt and pyproject.toml—since no async tests exist and none are planned. (2) Alternatively, if async testing is planned for future phases, add pytest-asyncio>=0.23.7,<1 to requirements-ci.txt and update pyproject.toml [dev] to match, restoring parity. Recommend option 1 (removal) since zero evidence of async test usage exists in the codebase. Add an upper bound to any remaining test dependencies: if kept, change to pytest-asyncio>=0.23.7,<1 to bound the major version.

content.content: Structured output provided successfully
